macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Hits Record High as US-Iran Ceasefire Continues
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed at a record high amid ongoing US-Iran ceasefire, boosting stock market sentiment. Stocks soared and oil prices plunged following the ceasefire announcement.

items.title: Federal Reserve Beige Book: NY District Activity Declines Modestly Amid Tariff and Middle East Uncertainty
items.summary: Economic activity in the Federal Reserve's Second District declined modestly due to tariff policy shifts and Middle East conflict, with steady employment, modest wage growth, and rising input prices especially in energy and materials.
items.source: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

items.title: March CPI Spikes 0.9% Led by 10.9% Energy Price Jump Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: US consumer prices rose sharply by 0.9% in March, pushing year-over-year CPI to 3.3% from 2.4%, driven by surging energy costs from the Iran conflict and potential Strait of Hormuz issues.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Drop to 6.3% Lowest in Four Weeks as Home Sales Season Heats Up
items.summary: Average 30-year fixed mortgage rates fell to 6.3%, the lowest in four weeks, supporting increased home sales activity.

items.title: PepsiCo Sales Jump After Price Cuts
items.summary: PepsiCo reported a sales increase following recent price reductions, highlighting positive company earnings amid economic pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $257M in Liquidations Across Crypto Markets in 24 Hours
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets experienced significant turbulence with $257 million in liquidations over 24 hours, with long positions hit hardest at $163 million. Bitcoin and Ethereum led losses at $55.59 million and $43.59 million respectively, affecting over 129,000 traders.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Files for Bitcoin ETF
items.summary: Goldman Sachs filed its first crypto-related exchange-traded fund on April 14, proposing a yield-focused, options-overlaid vehicle designed to hold spot Bitcoin ETFs from other issuers and generate income in normal or sideways markets.

items.title: Kraken Advances Confidential IPO Filing
items.summary: Kraken continues its confidential initial public offering with the SEC despite recent corporate valuation declines. Co-CEO Arjun Sethi confirmed the IPO ambitions, revealing regulatory paperwork was submitted in November 2025.

items.title: Bitcoin and Major Altcoins Show Mixed Price Movement
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $75,049.79, up 0.9% over 24 hours as of April 15. XRP rose 2.9% to $1.40 and Solana gained 1.8% to $85.51, while Ethereum increased 1.71% in the same period.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Managed Agents in Public Beta and Expands Compute Partnerships
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Managed Agents in public beta, Claude for Word, and details on Claude Mythos Preview, while expanding partnerships with Google and Broadcom for gigawatts of compute. Their run rate reportedly surged from $9B to $30B in four months.[1]

items.title: Evidence Mounts of Claude Opus 4.6 Performance Degradation
items.summary: Benchmarks show a 15-point drop in accuracy on BridgeBench hallucination tests and collapsed median thinking length from ~2,200 to ~600 characters. Hosts and users report similar experiences.[1]

items.title: Google Releases Gemini Subagents, CLI Updates, Native Mac App, and New TTS Model
items.summary: Google launched Gemini subagents for CLI, a native Mac app, new text-to-speech model, and prepay billing for the API, enabling parallel agent teams for research, coding, and bug fixing.[2][3]

items.title: OpenAI Updates Agents SDK with Sandboxing and Enhanced Harness
items.summary: OpenAI added native sandbox execution and a more capable harness to its Agents SDK, improving agent reliability and deployment.[2]

items.title: MiniMax Ships MMX CLI and Open-Sources M2.7 Model
items.summary: MiniMax released MMX CLI tool and open-sourced the M2.7 model, alongside GLM-5.1 entering Code Arena's top 3.[1]

items.title: Ramp AI Unveils Internal Productivity Suite and Latent Briefing Research
items.summary: Ramp launched Glass, its AI coworker suite, and published a Latent Briefing paper demonstrating 31% token savings in multi-agent systems.[1]

items.title: Scale AI's Muse Spark Now Powers Meta AI
items.summary: Scale AI's Muse Spark model is now integrated to power Meta AI capabilities.[1]

items.title: Widespread Benchmark Cheating Exposed in Nine Agent Benchmarks
items.summary: Investigations reveal cheating across nine agent benchmarks, impacting reliability of performance claims.[1]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Highly Anticipated Pirate Game Launches in Early Access
items.summary: The best pirate game, praised in a demo by streamers like Asmongold, has officially released in early access. Players can now dive into its seafaring adventures.

items.title: Path of Exile League 6 Releases with Smooth Launch and Quick Fixes
items.summary: League 6 for Path of Exile launched successfully, only 15 minutes late, despite initial bugs with items like ventor bow and bone crusher, which Jagex promptly fixed. Streamers are celebrating the major update.

items.title: Wrath: Aeon of Ruin VR Edition Now Available
items.summary: The brutal old-school boomer shooter Wrath: Aeon of Ruin, reminiscent of DOOM and Quake, is now playable in VR with its retro pixelated art style highlighted as a strength for the medium.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Nvidia Launches IceSing Open Models for Quantum Computing
items.summary: Nvidia has launched IceSing, the world's first open AI models for quantum computing, enabling up to 2.5 times faster and three times more accurate error correction. It auto-calibrates quantum processors in hours instead of days and is already used by top labs like Harvard, Fermilab, and Berkeley.[1]

items.title: Wise Tech Global Cuts Nearly 2,000 Jobs Amid Tech Shifts
items.summary: Australian company Wise Tech Global is cutting close to 2,000 jobs as part of broader tech industry changes. Atlassian has also announced massive job cuts.[2]
